content.full_text:
OFFICE OF THE COUNTY COUNSEL
COUNTY OF SUTTER
RONALD S. ERICKSON
DARRELL W. LARSEN
1160 Civic Center Boulevard
Assistant
County Counsel
Yuba City, California 95993
JAMES SCANLON
Telephone (916) 741-7110
JOSEPH P. CERULLO
DONALD F. STANTON
September 30, 1991
Deputies

Fair Political Practices
Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Gentlemen:

Sutter County is considering sewage disposal and domestic water
supply options for the unincorporated urban area adjacent to Yuba
City. An area has been identified and designated as a policy area.
For that policy area, the following policies have been established:
a) a master plan for sewage and water is to be developed by July 1,
1992; b) after January 1, 1993 no new tentative subdivision maps
will be approved without the provision of public sewer and water;
c) tentative map approvals between the date of the policy adoption,
February of 1990, and January 1, 1993 can occur if the subdivision
has installed lines which may be used for ultimate connection to
public sewer.

Twenty-eight percent of the current Sutter County population
(19,000 out of a total of 67,000) lives within the defined policy
area. Included are two supervisors, the county public works
director, and the county counsel.

While discrete areas within the policy area have unique water and
sewage issues associated with them reflecting different
characteristics such as groundwater quality, septic system
suitability, distance from public sewage and water services and the
extent of existing development, staff has recommended and the board
of supervisors has adopted an integrated approach to addressing all
water and sewage problems within the study area. That is, taking
one step with regard to sewage disposal in one portion of the policy area impacts what can and should be done in the balance of
the area and therefore all decisions, including regional ones, must
contemplate the whole. For instance, to approve a community sewage
system serving one subdivision only will impact the feasibility of
providing public service for the balance of the policy area as a
consequence of the increased cost resulting from a reduced number
of users over which the cost can be spread. The subdivision with
its own sewer system will not contribute to defraying the cost of
an area-wide sewage system.

Fair Political Practices
Commission
September 30, 1991
Page 2


All decisions regarding water and sewer within the study area must,
under the adopted policy, consider not only issues arising out of
the immediate area being considered, but also the impacts of the
decision on the entire study area.  Against this background we
inquire regarding whether there is a prohibited conflict of
interest when the supervisors and staff mentioned above participate
in or potentially influence decisions made on neighborhood, or
regional, or policy area-wide bases.  We have assumed, for the
purposes of this inquiry, that these types of decisions can have a
material financial effect on properties owned by these officials,
primarily their residences.  That is, improving the type of sewage
service to a residential lot, for instance by enabling public
sewers rather than septic systems, can significantly increase real
estate values.

We believe the "public generally" exception from prohibited
conflicts is applicable here based in part on Commission Opinion
76-005.  We make this request for guidance pursuant to and as
contemplated by Government Code section 83114(b).  Your assistance
in this matter will be appreciated.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
DARRELL W. LARSEN
SUTTER COUNTY COUNSEL

DWL:js

sections.question: Against this background we inquire regarding whether there is a prohibited conflict of interest when the supervisors and staff mentioned above participate in or potentially influence decisions made on neighborhood, or regional, or policy area-wide bases. we have assumed, for the purposes of this inquiry, that these types of decisions can have a material financial effect on properties orrned by these officials, primarily their residences.
sections.question_synthetic: Does Sutter County violate conflict of interest rules when county supervisors and staff who reside in a policy area participate in decisions about sewage and water systems that could materially affect their property values?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion section present. The document describes a policy area covering 28% of Sutter County's population and asks about applicability of the 'public generally' exception to conflict of interest rules under Government Code section 8314(b), referencing Commission Opinion 75-005.

embedding.summary: Sutter County Counsel Darrell W. Larsen requests FPPC guidance on whether supervisors and county staff who live in a designated policy area face prohibited conflicts of interest when participating in decisions about sewage and water infrastructure that could affect their residential property values.
